elements.name: intro_selfpaced_pd_equity_post
elements.html:
Equity in CS Self-paced Online Professional Learning for Educators

Post-Module Survey

Code.org relies on surveys as a crucial tool to stay connected to teachers, gather feedback about our curriculum and your teaching experience, and to improve our programs.

Privacy and Confidentiality

  • Your individual responses here are completely confidential.
  • Your identity will never be revealed publicly or used to tie any responses back to you personally.
  • We may share anonymous, aggregate data (representing all responses) publicly.
  • Your anonymized responses will be used by Code.org for program improvement.

Your honesty and candor are appreciated.

Equity in CS - Post-Module Survey

elements.name: better_understand_impact
elements.title: After completing this module, I have a better understanding of how systemic barriers, biases, and identity impact student experiences in CS.

elements.name: confidence_create_inclusive_classroom
elements.title: After completing this module,  I feel more confident in my ability to create an inclusive computer science classroom than I did before starting this professional learning course.

elements.name: content_new
elements.title: This module introduced me to new concepts and strategies for creating inclusive CS classrooms

elements.name: better_understand_equity_concepts
elements.title: After completing this module, I have a better understanding of...

rows.text: The definition of equity, diversity, and inclusion in CS education
Systemic barriers and biases that impact underrepresented groups in CS.
How students' diverse identities can influence their experiences and sense of belonging in CS.

elements.name: learning_obj_equity_strategies
elements.title: After completing this module, I feel confident to implement the following practices in my classroom:

rows.text: Concrete pedagogical approaches and strategies to engage diverse learners.
Practical tools and techniques for creating an inclusive classroom environment.
Strategies I can use to identify and mitigate implicit bias and stereotypes in the classroom.
Strategies to empower students from underrepresented groups to pursue CS.

elements.name: plan_use_content_learned
elements.title: Are you planning to use what you learned in this module?

choices.text: Yes, I already did
Yes, I plan to use what I learned
No, I didn’t find it relevant to my classroom/role

elements.name: barriers_implementation
elements.title: What, if any, are the main barriers to using what you’ve learned?

choices.text: None
I am not currently teaching computer science.
It is not clear to me how to apply what I’ve learned.
The strategies I learned are still new to me.
I cannot implement these strategies due to school/district policies.
I lack the time to implement these strategies.
I’m not sure how to start.
I need more examples that are relevant to my grade level/subject area.

elements.description: Rank the options from most helpful (5) to least helpful (1).

rows.text: Reading a page that contained text and images about a topic.
Making a plan to foster equitable access, diversity, and inclusion in my CS classroom.

elements.name: other_feedback
elements.title: Do you have any feedback about this module that you would like to share with the Code.org team?

elements.name: permission_to_quote
elements.title: I give Code.org permission to quote my written comments from this survey for use on social media, promotional materials, and other communications.

choices.text: Yes, I give Code.org permission to quote me and use my name.
Yes, I give Code.org permission to quote me, but I want to be anonymous.
No, I do not give Code.org my permission.

elements.name: equity_participate_focus_group
elements.title: OPTIONAL: Would you be willing to participate in a follow-up focus group to provide direct feedback about this online course?

choices.text: Yes
No
